question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setSymbol()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token symbol update with a value that, after DomainUtils.sanitize() processing, becomes an empty string, causing the history table to record a symbol change to '' that makes the token appear to have had no symbol during that history period?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setSymbol()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token symbol update with a very long string that DomainUtils.sanitize() truncates, causing the history row to store a truncated symbol that differs from the actual on-chain symbol, making the symbol history appear to show a different value than what was actually set?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken (Upsertable history=true)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the @Upsertable(history=true) upsert mechanism by submitting a token update with only a timestampRange change and no other field changes, causing a spurious history row to be written that makes the token appear to have had a state change at that timestamp when no actual state change occurred?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken (Upsertable skipPartialUpdate=true)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the skipPartialUpdate=true behavior of @Upsertable to submit a partial token update that is silently skipped, causing the history table to have a gap at a timestamp where a state change was submitted but not recorded, making the history appear incomplete?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken (Upsertable history=true)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update that triggers the upsert to move the current token row to token_history with an incorrect timestampRange upper bound, causing the history row to show a state as valid for a longer or shorter period than it actually was?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.feeScheduleKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token fee schedule update with a malformed byte[] feeScheduleKey that is stored in the history row, causing history consumers that parse the key to fail or misinterpret the fee schedule history, corrupting the visible fee configuration timeline?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.freezeKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a zero-length byte[] freezeKey, causing the history row to record a freeze key removal event that makes the token appear to have had its freeze key deleted at that timestamp, even if the actual on-chain state was different?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.kycKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a null kycKey that, due to the skipPartialUpdate=true behavior, is treated as 'no change' and causes the history row to retain the old kycKey value, making the history appear to show the key was never changed when it actually was?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.wipeKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a crafted wipeKey byte[] that is identical to a known key from another token, causing the history row to record a wipe key that appears to belong to a different token's key set, corrupting the key assignment history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.supplyKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a supplyKey byte[] of maximum allowed size, causing the history row insertion to fail or be truncated, resulting in a corrupted history entry at that timestamp that breaks the supply key change timeline?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.pauseKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a pauseKey byte[] set to all-zero bytes, causing the history row to record a pause key that appears valid but is cryptographically meaningless, making the pause key history show a key assignment that was never functionally valid?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.metadata] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token metadata update with a byte[] containing arbitrary data, causing the history row to record metadata that, when interpreted by downstream consumers, appears to reference a different token standard or NFT collection, corrupting the metadata provenance history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.metadataKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a metadataKey byte[] that is null when the previous history row had a non-null metadataKey, exploiting the skipPartialUpdate=true behavior to cause the history row to show the key as still present when it was actually removed, corrupting the metadata key history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.tokenId] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token event with a tokenId that collides with an existing token's ID (e.g., via entity ID reuse after deletion), causing the upsert to overwrite the existing token's current state and push a false history row that merges two distinct tokens' histories?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.tokenId] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Id annotation on tokenId — which is a Long — to submit a token event with tokenId=0 or tokenId=Long.MIN_VALUE, causing the upsert to create a history row under an invalid token ID that pollutes the history table and confuses range queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TokenHistoryRepository.prune()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a token operation that causes the prune() method's `timestamp_range << int8range(?1, null)` condition to delete history rows that are still needed for audit purposes, by manipulating the consensusTimestamp parameter through a crafted transaction that advances the pruning threshold?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TokenHistoryRepository.prune()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a very large consensusTimestamp that, when used as the prune threshold, causes all existing token_history rows to be deleted, permanently erasing the token's transaction history without any direct fund movement?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.timestampRange] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a timestampRange whose lower bound is Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the history row to be stored with an effectively infinite start time that makes it appear as the most recent state in all range queries, reordering the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.timestampRange] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update with a timestampRange of Range.all() (unbounded on both sides), causing the history row to overlap with all other history rows for the same token, making range-based history queries return this row for every possible timestamp and effectively hiding all other history entries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.timestampRange] Can an unprivileged external user submit two token updates with identical timestampRange lower bounds, causing the upsert to produce two history rows with the same range start, making it impossible for consumers to determine which state was valid at that timestamp and corrupting the history ordering?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken (SuperBuilder)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SuperBuilder(toBuilder=true) pattern — which allows building a Token from an existing instance — to craft a token update that copies all fields from a different token's state and submits it as an update to the target token, causing the history to show the target token adopting another token's complete state?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.freezeStatus] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update that sets freezeStatus to an ordinal value outside the valid TokenFreezeStatusEnum range, causing the history row to store an invalid freeze state that, when read back, either throws an exception or maps to an unexpected enum constant, corrupting the freeze history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.kycStatus] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update that sets kycStatus to ordinal 0 (which may map to a default/unset state) when the token was previously in a GRANTED state, causing the history row to show a KYC revocation that never occurred on-chain, corrupting the KYC grant/revoke history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.totalSupply (UpsertColumn)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token mint event with totalSupply=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the UpsertColumn coalesce expression to overflow when added to the existing supply, producing a negative totalSupply in the history row that makes the supply history appear to show a massive burn that never occurred?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AbstractToken.totalSupply (UpsertColumn)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token burn event with totalSupply=Long.MIN_VALUE, causing the UpsertColumn coalesce expression `e_{0} + coalesce({0}, {1})` to underflow, producing a history row with a totalSupply that wraps around to a large positive value, making the supply history show a phantom mint?
